content:
# Exodus Chapter 9 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~36r–37r (PDF pp. 69–70); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 49–50 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_009_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_009_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_009_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_009_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

## Plague of Livestock Disease — Dever (vv. 1–7)

**1** And YHWH said to Mosheh: "Go in to Par'oh, and you shall speak to him, 'Thus says YHWH, the God of the Ivrim [Hebrews]: Send out my people, and let them serve me.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה בֹּא אֶל־פַּרְעֹה וְדִבַּרְתָּ אֵלָיו כֹּה־אָמַר יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵי הָעִבְרִים שַׁלַּח אֶת־עַמִּי וְיַעַבְדֻנִי (bo el-Par'oh ve-dibbarta elav, "go in to Par'oh and you shall speak to him"; Elohei ha-Ivrim, "the God of the Hebrews"; shalakh et-ammi ve-ya'avduni, "send out my people and let them serve me"). **Note:** The formula now includes "God of the Hebrews" (אֱלֹהֵי הָעִבְרִים), as in 7:16 — an ethnic-political identifier for the Egyptian court. LXX (Vaticanus): εἴσελθε πρὸς Φαραω καὶ ἐρεῖς αὐτῷ· τάδε λέγει κύριος ὁ θεὸς τῶν Εβραίων· ἐξαπόστειλον τὸν λαόν μου ἵνα μοι λατρεύσωσιν ("go in to Pharao and say to him: thus says the Lord the God of the Hebrews: send out my people that they may serve me").

**2** For if you [are] refusing to send out, and you still hold them strongly [chazaq],

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כִּי אִם־מָאֵן אַתָּה לְשַׁלֵּחַ וְעוֹדְךָ מַחֲזִיק בָּם (im-ma'en attah le-shale'akh, "if you [are] refusing to send out"; ve-odekha makhaziq bam, "and you still hold them strongly/seize upon them"). **Hardening language note:** The verb מַחֲזִיק (makhaziq) is the hiphil participle of חזק (chazaq) — the same root used for heart-hardening, here meaning "to hold fast, grasp strongly." Par'oh's "holding" of the people mirrors his heart's "strengthening." LXX: εἰ μὲν οὖν μὴ βούλει ἐξαποστεῖλαι... ἔτι δὲ ἐγκρατεῖς αὐτῶν ("if you do not wish to send out... but still hold power over them") — ἐγκρατής ("holding power over") captures the chazaq hiphil force.

**3** behold, the hand of YHWH is upon your livestock which [is] in the field — upon the horses, upon the donkeys, upon the camels, upon the cattle, and upon the sheep — a dever [pestilence] very heavy [kaved].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads הִנֵּה יַד־יְהוָה הוֹיָה בְּמִקְנְךָ אֲשֶׁר בַּשָּׂדֶה בַּסּוּסִים בַּחֲמֹרִים בַּגְּמַלִּים בַּבָּקָר וּבַצֹּאן דֶּבֶר כָּבֵד מְאֹד (yad-YHWH hoyah be-miqnekha, "the hand of YHWH is/will be upon your livestock"; ba-susim ba-khamorim ba-gemalim ba-baqar u-va-tson, "upon the horses, upon the donkeys, upon the camels, upon the cattle, and upon the sheep"; dever kaved me'od, "a pestilence very heavy"). The word דֶּבֶר (dever) = "pestilence, plague, murrain" — from the root דבר which in its basic sense means "speak/word," but in this nominal form denotes deadly plague. Note כָּבֵד (kaved) = "heavy" used to describe the plague's severity — the same root as the hardening verb. LXX: ἰδοὺ χεὶρ κυρίου ἐπέσται ἐπὶ τὰ κτήνη σου τὰ ἐν τοῖς πεδίοις... θάνατος μέγας σφόδρα ("behold, the hand of the Lord shall be upon your cattle in the fields... a very great death") — θάνατος ("death") for dever.

**4** And YHWH will distinguish between the livestock of Yisra'el and the livestock of Mitsrayim, and not a thing shall die from all [that belongs] to the sons of Yisra'el.'"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהִפְלָה יְהוָה בֵּין מִקְנֵה יִשְׂרָאֵל וּבֵין מִקְנֵה מִצְרַיִם וְלֹא יָמוּת מִכָּל־לִבְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל דָּבָר (ve-hiflah YHWH bein miqneh Yisra'el u-vein miqneh Mitsrayim, "and YHWH will distinguish between the livestock of Yisra'el and the livestock of Mitsrayim"; ve-lo yamut mi-kol li-vnei Yisra'el davar, "and not a thing shall die from all belonging to the sons of Yisra'el"). **Goshen/Israel distinction:** The verb הִפְלָה (hiflah), hiphil of פלה (palah, "to set apart, distinguish"), continues the theme from 8:22 — YHWH actively separates the fate of Yisra'el from Mitsrayim. Note the wordplay: דָּבָר (davar, "thing/word") echoes דֶּבֶר (dever, "pestilence"). LXX: καὶ παραδοξάσω ἐγὼ ἐν τῷ καιρῷ ἐκείνῳ ἀνὰ μέσον τῶν κτηνῶν τῶν Αἰγυπτίων καὶ ἀνὰ μέσον τῶν κτηνῶν τῶν υἱῶν Ισραηλ ("and I will make wondrous at that time between the cattle of the Egyptians and the cattle of the sons of Israel") — παραδοξάζω for hiflah, as in 8:22.

**5** And YHWH set a mo'ed [appointed time], saying: "Tomorrow YHWH will do this thing in the land."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיָּשֶׂם יְהוָה מוֹעֵד לֵאמֹר מָחָר יַעֲשֶׂה יְהוָה הַדָּבָר הַזֶּה בָּאָרֶץ (vayasem YHWH mo'ed, "and YHWH set an appointed time"; makhar ya'aseh YHWH ha-davar ha-zeh ba-arets, "tomorrow YHWH will do this thing in the land"). The word מוֹעֵד (mo'ed) = "appointed time, meeting, season" — from יעד ("to appoint"), the same word used for the Tabernacle festivals and the Tent of Meeting (ohel mo'ed). YHWH sets a precise time for judgment. LXX: καὶ ἔδωκεν ὁ θεὸς ὅρον λέγων ("and God gave a limit/boundary, saying") — ὅρος ("limit, boundary") for mo'ed.

**6** And YHWH did this thing on the next day, and all the livestock of Mitsrayim died; and from the livestock of the sons of Yisra'el not one died.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעַשׂ יְהוָה אֶת־הַדָּבָר הַזֶּה מִמָּחֳרָת וַיָּמָת כֹּל מִקְנֵה מִצְרָיִם וּמִמִּקְנֵה בְנֵי־יִשְׂרָאֵל לֹא־מֵת אֶחָד (vayamat kol miqneh Mitsrayim, "and all the livestock of Mitsrayim died"; u-mi-miqneh venei-Yisra'el lo-met ekhad, "and from the livestock of the sons of Yisra'el not one died"). The "all" (כֹּל) is hyperbolic or refers to all livestock "in the field" (v.3) — livestock reappear later in the hail narrative (v.19), indicating not literally every animal perished. LXX: καὶ ἐτελεύτησεν πάντα τὰ κτήνη τῶν Αἰγυπτίων· ἀπὸ δὲ τῶν κτηνῶν τῶν υἱῶν Ισραηλ οὐκ ἐτελεύτησεν οὐδέν ("and all the cattle of the Egyptians died; but of the cattle of the sons of Israel not one died").

**7** And Par'oh sent, and behold, not even one of the livestock of Yisra'el had died; and the heart of Par'oh was heavy [kaved], and he did not send out the people.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּשְׁלַח פַּרְעֹה וְהִנֵּה לֹא־מֵת מִמִּקְנֵה יִשְׂרָאֵל עַד־אֶחָד וַיִּכְבַּד לֵב פַּרְעֹה וְלֹא שִׁלַּח אֶת־הָעָם (vayishlakh Par'oh, "and Par'oh sent [to investigate]"; ad-ekhad, "up to one" i.e. "not even one"; vayikhbad lev Par'oh, "and the heart of Par'oh was heavy"). **Hardening verb: kaved (כבד) = "heavy/be heavy."** The form וַיִּכְבַּד (vayikhbad) is qal — Par'oh's heart "was heavy" or "became heavy," a stative description. Par'oh investigates, confirms the distinction, and still refuses. LXX: καὶ ἐβαρύνθη ἡ καρδία Φαραω ("and the heart of Pharao was made heavy") — βαρύνω passive for kaved qal.

---

## Plague of Boils — Shechin (vv. 8–12)

**8** And YHWH said to Mosheh and to Aharon: "Take for yourselves full hands of piakh [soot] of a kiln, and Mosheh shall scatter it toward the heavens before the eyes of Par'oh.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה וְאֶל־אַהֲרֹן קְחוּ לָכֶם מְלֹא חָפְנֵיכֶם פִּיחַ כִּבְשָׁן וּזְרָקוֹ מֹשֶׁה הַשָּׁמַיְמָה לְעֵינֵי פַרְעֹה (qekhu lakhem melo khofneikhem pi'akh kivshan, "take for yourselves full [of] your fists soot [of] a kiln"; u-zeraqo Mosheh ha-shamaimah, "and Mosheh shall scatter it toward the heavens"; le-einei Far'oh, "before the eyes of Par'oh"). The word פִּיחַ (pi'akh) = "soot, fine ash" — hapax-like usage in plague context. כִּבְשָׁן (kivshan) = "kiln, smelting furnace." The action reverses Egyptian kiln-craft: the furnace that enslaved Yisra'el in brick-making now becomes the source of plague. LXX: λάβετε ὑμεῖς πλήρεις τὰς χεῖρας αἰθάλης καμιναίας ("take for yourselves full hands of furnace-soot") — αἰθάλη = "soot," κάμινος = "furnace."

**9** And it shall become fine dust upon all the land of Mitsrayim, and it shall become upon the adam [man/humanity] and upon the beast a shechin [boil] sprouting with aba'bu'ot [blisters/pustules] in all the land of Mitsrayim."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהָיָה לְאָבָק עַל כָּל־אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם וְהָיָה עַל־הָאָדָם וְעַל־הַבְּהֵמָה לִשְׁחִין פֹּרֵחַ אֲבַעְבֻּעֹת בְּכָל־אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם (ve-hayah le-avaq, "and it shall become fine dust"; li-shekhin pore'akh ava'bu'ot, "for a boil sprouting blisters/pustules"). The word שְׁחִין (shekhin) = "boil, skin inflammation" — same word used for the sixth plague and later in Devarim 28:27, 35 and Iyyov 2:7. אֲבַעְבֻּעֹת (ava'bu'ot) = "blisters, pustules" — from a reduplicated root (בעבע), suggesting bubbling/blistering. LXX: καὶ ἔσται... ἕλκη φλυκτίδες ἀναζέουσαι ("and it shall become... sores, blisters boiling up") — φλυκτίς = "blister," ἀναζέω = "boil up."

**10** And they took the soot of the kiln, and they stood before Par'oh, and Mosheh scattered it toward the heavens, and it became a shechin [boil] of blisters sprouting on the adam [man/humanity] and on the beast.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּקְחוּ אֶת־פִּיחַ הַכִּבְשָׁן וַיַּעַמְדוּ לִפְנֵי פַרְעֹה וַיִּזְרֹק אֹתוֹ מֹשֶׁה הַשָּׁמָיְמָה וַיְהִי שְׁחִין אֲבַעְבֻּעֹת פֹּרֵחַ בָּאָדָם וּבַבְּהֵמָה (vayizroq oto Mosheh ha-shamaimah, "and Mosheh scattered it toward the heavens"; vayehi shekhin ava'bu'ot pore'akh, "and it became a boil of blisters sprouting"). The act is performed publicly — le-einei Far'oh ("before Par'oh's eyes," v.8) — a direct challenge to Egyptian power. LXX: καὶ ἔρριψεν αὐτὴν Μωυσῆς εἰς τὸν οὐρανόν ("and Moses threw it into the heaven").

**11** And the chartummim [magicians] were not able to stand before Mosheh because of the shechin [boil], for the shechin was on the chartummim and on all Mitsrayim.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְלֹא־יָכְלוּ הַחַרְטֻמִּים לַעֲמֹד לִפְנֵי מֹשֶׁה מִפְּנֵי הַשְּׁחִין כִּי־הָיָה הַשְּׁחִין בַּחַרְטֻמִּם וּבְכָל־מִצְרָיִם (ve-lo yakhlu ha-khartummim la'amod lifnei Mosheh, "and the chartummim were not able to stand before Mosheh"; mippenei ha-shekhin, "because of the boil"; ki-hayah ha-shekhin ba-khartummim, "for the boil was on the chartummim"). **Narrative escalation:** The chartummim, who could not replicate the kinnim (8:18), are now themselves struck by the plague. They cannot even appear before Mosheh. This is their final appearance in the plague narrative — they are defeated and afflicted. LXX: οὐκ ἠδύναντο οἱ φαρμακοὶ στῆναι ἐναντίον Μωυσῆ διὰ τὰ ἕλκη ("the sorcerers were not able to stand before Moses because of the sores").

**12** And YHWH strengthened [chazaq] the heart of Par'oh, and he did not listen to them, as YHWH had spoken to Mosheh.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְחַזֵּק יְהוָה אֶת־לֵב פַּרְעֹה וְלֹא שָׁמַע אֲלֵהֶם כַּאֲשֶׁר דִּבֶּר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה (vayekhazzeq YHWH et-lev Par'oh, "and YHWH strengthened the heart of Par'oh"; ve-lo shama' alehem, "and he did not listen to them"; ka-asher dibber YHWH el-Mosheh, "as YHWH had spoken to Mosheh").
>
> **CRITICAL — HARDENING VERB: chazaq (חזק) = "strengthen." PIEL וַיְחַזֵּק — YHWH is the AGENT.** This is the TURNING POINT in the hardening narrative. For the first time in the plague sequence, YHWH is the grammatical subject who actively strengthens (chazaq piel) Par'oh's heart. Previously: Par'oh's heart "was strengthened" (chazaq qal, 7:13, 7:22, 8:19) or Par'oh "made heavy" his own heart (kaved hiphil, 8:15, 8:32) or Par'oh's heart "was heavy" (kaved qal, 9:7). Now YHWH acts directly. The piel stem is intensive/causative — YHWH actively and intensively strengthens Par'oh's resistance. This fulfills the prediction of 7:3 (though 7:3 uses qashah, not chazaq) and 4:21 (chazaq piel: "I will strengthen his heart"). The theological weight is immense: after Par'oh has repeatedly chosen hardness on his own, YHWH now confirms and intensifies that trajectory.
>
> LXX: καὶ ἐσκλήρυνεν κύριος τὴν καρδίαν Φαραω ("and the Lord hardened the heart of Pharao") — σκληρύνω ("harden") with κύριος as subject, clearly marking divine agency.

---

## Plague of Hail — Barad (vv. 13–35)

**13** And YHWH said to Mosheh: "Rise early in the morning and stand before Par'oh, and you shall say to him, 'Thus says YHWH, the God of the Ivrim [Hebrews]: Send out my people, and let them serve me.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה הַשְׁכֵּם בַּבֹּקֶר וְהִתְיַצֵּב לִפְנֵי פַרְעֹה וְאָמַרְתָּ אֵלָיו כֹּה־אָמַר יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵי הָעִבְרִים שַׁלַּח אֶת־עַמִּי וְיַעַבְדֻנִי (hashkem ba-boqer ve-hityatsev lifnei Far'oh, "rise early in the morning and station yourself before Par'oh"). This echoes 8:20 — the same morning confrontation formula. LXX: ὄρθρισον τὸ πρωῒ καὶ στῆθι ἐναντίον Φαραω ("rise early in the morning and stand before Pharao").

**14** For at this time I [am] sending all my plagues to your heart, and upon your servants, and upon your people, so that you may know that there is none like me in all the land.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כִּי בַּפַּעַם הַזֹּאת אֲנִי שֹׁלֵחַ אֶת־כָּל־מַגֵּפֹתַי אֶל־לִבְּךָ וּבַעֲבָדֶיךָ וּבְעַמֶּךָ בַּעֲבוּר תֵּדַע כִּי אֵין כָּמֹנִי בְּכָל־הָאָרֶץ (ba-pa'am ha-zo't ani shole'akh et-kol-maggefotai el-libbekha, "at this time I [am] sending all my plagues to your heart"; ba-avur teda', "so that you may know"; ki ein kamoni be-khol-ha-arets, "that there is none like me in all the land"). **Key phrase:** אֶל־לִבְּךָ (el-libbekha, "to your heart") — the plagues are directed at Par'oh's heart, the very organ that has been hardened. The word מַגֵּפֹתַי (maggefotai) = "my plagues/strikes" from נגף ("to strike"). LXX: ἐν τῷ γὰρ νῦν καιρῷ ἐγὼ ἐξαποστέλλω πάντα τὰ συναντήματά μου εἰς τὴν καρδίαν σου ("for at this present time I send forth all my calamities into your heart") — συνάντημα = "calamity/occurrence" for maggefah.

**15** For now I could have sent out my hand and struck you and your people with the dever [pestilence], and you would have been cut off from the land.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כִּי עַתָּה שָׁלַחְתִּי אֶת־יָדִי וָאַךְ אוֹתְךָ וְאֶת־עַמְּךָ בַּדָּבֶר וַתִּכָּחֵד מִן־הָאָרֶץ (ki attah shalakhti et-yadi va-akh otkha ve-et-ammekha ba-daver, "for now I sent out my hand and I could have struck you and your people with the pestilence"; va-tikkakhed min-ha-arets, "and you would have been hidden/cut off from the land"). The form שָׁלַחְתִּי (shalakhti) is a perfectum with hypothetical/potential force — "I could have sent." The verb תִּכָּחֵד (tikkakhed) from כחד (niph., "be hidden, destroyed, cut off") — total annihilation. LXX: νῦν γὰρ ἀποστείλας τὴν χεῖρά μου πατάξω σε καὶ τὸν λαόν σου θανάτῳ, καὶ ἐκτριβήσῃ ἀπὸ τῆς γῆς ("for now sending forth my hand I shall strike you and your people with death, and you shall be utterly destroyed from the land").

**16** And yet, for this [reason] I have caused you to stand: for the sake of showing you my strength, and so that my name may be told in all the land.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאוּלָם בַּעֲבוּר זֹאת הֶעֱמַדְתִּיךָ בַּעֲבוּר הַרְאֹתְךָ אֶת־כֹּחִי וּלְמַעַן סַפֵּר שְׁמִי בְּכָל־הָאָרֶץ (ve-ulam ba-avur zo't he'emadtikha, "and yet for this [reason] I have caused you to stand"; ba-avur har'otkha et-kokhi, "for the sake of showing you my strength"; u-le-ma'an sapper shemi be-khol-ha-arets, "and so that my name may be told in all the land"). **Key theological verse:** הֶעֱמַדְתִּיךָ (he'emadtikha) = hiphil of עמד, "I have caused you to stand/raised you up" — YHWH has sustained Par'oh in existence for a purpose. This is the verse Paul quotes in Romans 9:17. The purpose is dual: (1) to display divine power, and (2) to make YHWH's name known universally. LXX: καὶ ἕνεκεν τούτου διετηρήθης ἵνα ἐνδείξωμαι ἐν σοὶ τὴν ἰσχύν μου ("and for this reason you have been preserved, that I may display in you my strength") — διατηρέω ("preserve/keep alive") interprets he'emadtikha.

**17** Still you [are] exalting yourself against my people, by not sending them out.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads עוֹדְךָ מִסְתּוֹלֵל בְּעַמִּי לְבִלְתִּי שַׁלְּחָם (odekha mistolel be-ammi le-vilti shalkham, "still you [are] exalting yourself against my people, by not sending them out"). The verb מִסְתּוֹלֵל (mistolel) is the hitpael of סלל (salal, "to heap up, exalt") — "you are raising yourself up, acting presumptuously." LXX: σὺ ἐμποιῇ τοῦ λαοῦ μου τοῦ μὴ ἐξαποστεῖλαι αὐτούς ("you make yourself against my people so as not to send them out").

**18** Behold, about this time tomorrow I [am] causing to rain a very heavy [kaved] hail [barad], the like of which has not been in Mitsrayim from the day it was founded and until now.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads הִנְנִי מַמְטִיר כָּעֵת מָחָר בָּרָד כָּבֵד מְאֹד אֲשֶׁר לֹא־הָיָה כָמֹהוּ בְּמִצְרַיִם לְמִן־הַיּוֹם הִוָּסְדָה וְעַד־עָתָּה (hineni mamtir, "behold I [am] causing to rain"; ka-et makhar, "about this time tomorrow"; barad kaved me'od, "hail very heavy"; le-min-ha-yom hivvasdah, "from the day it was founded"). The adjective כָּבֵד (kaved, "heavy") describes the hail — the same root used of Par'oh's heart. בָּרָד (barad) = "hail." The founding reference (הִוָּסְדָה, hivvasdah, niphal of יסד, "to found") treats Mitsrayim as an established civilization whose entire history has seen nothing comparable. LXX: ἰδοὺ ἐγὼ ὕω ταύτην τὴν ὥραν αὔριον χάλαζαν πολλὴν σφόδρα ("behold, I rain at this hour tomorrow hail very much/great") — χάλαζα = "hail."

**19** And now, send, shelter your livestock and all that [belongs] to you in the field; every adam [man/person] and beast that is found in the field and is not gathered into the house — the hail shall come down upon them, and they shall die.'"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעַתָּה שְׁלַח הָעֵז אֶת־מִקְנְךָ וְאֵת כָּל־אֲשֶׁר לְךָ בַּשָּׂדֶה כָּל־הָאָדָם וְהַבְּהֵמָה אֲשֶׁר־יִמָּצֵא בַשָּׂדֶה וְלֹא יֵאָסֵף הַבַּיְתָה וְיָרַד עֲלֵהֶם הַבָּרָד וָמֵתוּ (shelakh ha'ez et-miqnekha, "send, shelter your livestock"; ha'ez from עוז, hiphil imperative, "cause to be strong/bring to safety/shelter"; kol-ha-adam ve-ha-behemah asher-yimmatse va-sadeh, "every person and beast that is found in the field"; ve-lo ye'asef ha-baitah, "and is not gathered to the house"). **Note:** This is an act of mercy embedded in the plague announcement — YHWH warns and gives Par'oh opportunity to protect lives. The word הָעֵז (ha'ez) = "shelter/bring to safety" — a rare usage. LXX: κάτασπευσον οὖν συναγαγεῖν τὰ κτήνη σου ("hasten therefore to gather your cattle").

**20** The one who feared the word of YHWH among the servants of Par'oh caused his servants and his livestock to flee into the houses.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads הַיָּרֵא אֶת־דְּבַר יְהוָה מֵעַבְדֵי פַּרְעֹה הֵנִיס אֶת־עֲבָדָיו וְאֶת־מִקְנֵהוּ אֶל־הַבָּתִּים (ha-yare et-devar YHWH me-avdei Far'oh, "the one who feared the word of YHWH from among the servants of Par'oh"; henis et-avadav ve-et-miqnehu el-ha-battim, "caused his servants and his livestock to flee into the houses"). **Significant:** Some Egyptians now fear YHWH's word — the plagues are having a cumulative effect on Mitsrayim's people, even if not on Par'oh. The verb הֵנִיס (henis) = hiphil of נוס, "caused to flee/drove [to safety]." LXX: ὁ φοβούμενος τὸ ῥῆμα κυρίου τῶν θεραπόντων Φαραω συνήγαγεν τὰ κτήνη αὐτοῦ εἰς τοὺς οἴκους ("the one fearing the word of the Lord among the servants of Pharao gathered his cattle into the houses").

**21** And the one who did not set his heart to the word of YHWH — he left his servants and his livestock in the field.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַאֲשֶׁר לֹא־שָׂם לִבּוֹ אֶל־דְּבַר יְהוָה וַיַּעֲזֹב אֶת־עֲבָדָיו וְאֶת־מִקְנֵהוּ בַּשָּׂדֶה (va-asher lo-sam libbo el-devar YHWH, "and the one who did not set his heart to the word of YHWH"; vaya'azov et-avadav ve-et-miqnehu ba-sadeh, "and he left his servants and his livestock in the field"). **Heart language:** לֹא־שָׂם לִבּוֹ (lo-sam libbo, "did not set his heart") — the heart that refuses to attend to YHWH's word mirrors Par'oh's hardened heart. Those who ignore the warning suffer the consequences. LXX: ὃς δὲ μὴ προσέσχεν τῇ διανοίᾳ εἰς τὸ ῥῆμα κυρίου ("but the one who did not direct his mind to the word of the Lord") — διάνοια ("mind/understanding") for lev.

**22** And YHWH said to Mosheh: "Stretch out your hand toward the heavens, and there shall be hail in all the land of Mitsrayim — upon the adam [man/humanity], and upon the beast, and upon every herb of the field in the land of Mitsrayim."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־מֹשֶׁה נְטֵה אֶת־יָדְךָ עַל־הַשָּׁמַיִם וִיהִי בָרָד בְּכָל־אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם עַל־הָאָדָם וְעַל־הַבְּהֵמָה וְעַל כָּל־עֵשֶׂב הַשָּׂדֶה בְּאֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם (neteh et-yadkha al-ha-shamayim, "stretch out your hand toward/over the heavens"; vihi varad be-khol-erets Mitsrayim, "and there shall be hail in all the land of Mitsrayim"). **Note:** Mosheh now acts alone (without Aharon's rod) — the progression from Aharon's agency to Mosheh's direct action continues. LXX: ἔκτεινον τὴν χεῖρά σου εἰς τὸν οὐρανόν ("stretch out your hand into the heaven").

**23** And Mosheh stretched out his rod toward the heavens, and YHWH gave thunders [qolot] and hail [barad], and fire went toward the land; and YHWH caused to rain hail upon the land of Mitsrayim.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֵּט מֹשֶׁה אֶת־מַטֵּהוּ עַל־הַשָּׁמַיִם וַיהוָה נָתַן קֹלֹת וּבָרָד וַתִּהֲלַךְ אֵשׁ אָרְצָה וַיַּמְטֵר יְהוָה בָּרָד עַל־אֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם (vayyet Mosheh et-mattehu al-ha-shamayim, "and Mosheh stretched out his rod toward the heavens"; va-YHWH natan qolot u-varad, "and YHWH gave thunders and hail"; va-tihalakh esh artsah, "and fire went toward the land"; vayamter YHWH barad, "and YHWH caused to rain hail"). The word קֹלֹת (qolot) = "voices/thunders" — from קוֹל (qol, "voice, sound"). Thunder is the "voice" of YHWH. The fire (אֵשׁ, esh) going to the land indicates lightning accompanying the hail. LXX: καὶ κύριος ἔδωκεν φωνὰς καὶ χάλαζαν, καὶ διέτρεχεν τὸ πῦρ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς ("and the Lord gave voices and hail, and fire ran upon the land") — φωνή = "voice" for qol.

**24** And there was hail, and fire taking hold of itself in the midst of the hail — very heavy [kaved] — the like of which had not been in all the land of Mitsrayim since it became a nation.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְהִי בָרָד וְאֵשׁ מִתְלַקַּחַת בְּתוֹךְ הַבָּרָד כָּבֵד מְאֹד אֲשֶׁר לֹא־הָיָה כָמֹהוּ בְּכָל־אֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם מֵאָז הָיְתָה לְגוֹי (ve-esh mitlaqa'khat be-tokh ha-barad, "and fire taking hold of itself in the midst of the hail" — the hitpael of לקח, "to take," describes fire "seizing/flashing" within the hailstones; kaved me'od, "very heavy"; me-az haytah le-goi, "since it became a nation"). The phenomenon of fire within ice is presented as supernatural — a merging of opposites. LXX: τὸ δὲ πῦρ φλογίζον ἐν τῇ χαλάζῃ ("and the fire blazing in the hail") — φλογίζω = "set ablaze."

**25** And the hail struck in all the land of Mitsrayim all that [was] in the field, from adam [man/humanity] and to beast; and every herb of the field the hail struck, and every tree of the field it broke.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּךְ הַבָּרָד בְּכָל־אֶרֶץ מִצְרַיִם אֵת כָּל־אֲשֶׁר בַּשָּׂדֶה מֵאָדָם וְעַד־בְּהֵמָה וְאֵת כָּל־עֵשֶׂב הַשָּׂדֶה הִכָּה הַבָּרָד וְאֶת־כָּל־עֵץ הַשָּׂדֶה שִׁבֵּר (vayakh ha-barad, "and the hail struck"; me-adam ve-ad-behemah, "from man and unto beast"; ve-et kol-ets ha-sadeh shibber, "and every tree of the field it broke"). The verb שִׁבֵּר (shibber) = piel of שבר, "broke/shattered" — intensively destroying the trees. LXX: καὶ ἐπάταξεν ἡ χάλαζα ἐν πάσῃ γῇ Αἰγύπτου ("and the hail struck in all the land of Egypt").

**26** Only in the land of Goshen, where the sons of Yisra'el [were], there was no hail.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads רַק בְּאֶרֶץ גֹּשֶׁן אֲשֶׁר־שָׁם בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל לֹא הָיָה בָּרָד (raq be-erets Goshen asher-sham benei Yisra'el lo hayah barad, "only in the land of Goshen where the sons of Yisra'el [were] there was no hail"). **Goshen distinction:** The separation continues from 8:22 and 9:4, 6 — the land of Goshen is explicitly protected. The adverb רַק (raq, "only, surely") marks the exception. LXX: πλὴν ἐν γῇ Γεσεμ, οὗ ἦσαν οἱ υἱοὶ Ισραηλ, οὐκ ἐγένετο ἡ χάλαζα ("except in the land of Gesem, where the sons of Israel were, the hail did not happen").

**27** And Par'oh sent and called to Mosheh and to Aharon and said to them: "I have sinned this time; YHWH [is] the righteous one, and I and my people [are] the wicked ones.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיִּשְׁלַח פַּרְעֹה וַיִּקְרָא לְמֹשֶׁה וּלְאַהֲרֹן וַיֹּאמֶר אֲלֵהֶם חָטָאתִי הַפָּעַם יְהוָה הַצַּדִּיק וַאֲנִי וְעַמִּי הָרְשָׁעִים (khatati ha-pa'am, "I have sinned this time"; YHWH ha-tsaddiq, "YHWH [is] the righteous one"; va-ani ve-ammi ha-resha'im, "and I and my people [are] the wicked ones"). **Par'oh's confession:** The first time Par'oh uses the language of sin (חָטָאתִי, khatati) and righteousness (צַדִּיק, tsaddiq) versus wickedness (רְשָׁעִים, resha'im). This is judicial/covenantal language. LXX: ἡμάρτηκα τὸ νῦν· ὁ κύριος δίκαιος, ἐγὼ δὲ καὶ ὁ λαός μου ἀσεβεῖς ("I have sinned now; the Lord is righteous, but I and my people are ungodly") — ἀσεβής ("impious/ungodly") for rasha'.

**28** Pray to YHWH, and [let there be] enough of the qolot [thunders] of God and hail; and I will send you out, and you shall not again stand [here]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads הַעְתִּירוּ אֶל־יְהוָה וְרַב מִהְיֹת קֹלֹת אֱלֹהִים וּבָרָד וַאֲשַׁלְּחָה אֶתְכֶם וְלֹא תֹסִפוּן לַעֲמֹד (ha'tiru el-YHWH, "pray to YHWH"; ve-rav mihyot qolot Elohim u-varad, "and [let there be] enough/ceasing from the thunders of God and hail"; va-ashalkhah etkhem, "and I will send you out"; ve-lo tosifun la'amod, "and you shall not again stand/remain"). The phrase קֹלֹת אֱלֹהִים (qolot Elohim) = "thunders of God" — the superlative use of Elohim (like "mountains of God" = "mighty mountains"). LXX: εὔξασθε πρὸς κύριον, καὶ παυσάσθω τοῦ γενηθῆναι φωνὰς θεοῦ καὶ χάλαζαν ("pray to the Lord, and let the voices of God and hail cease").

**29** And Mosheh said to him: "As I go out of the city, I will spread my palms to YHWH; the thunders [qolot] will cease, and the hail will be no more — so that you may know that the land [belongs] to YHWH.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר אֵלָיו מֹשֶׁה כְּצֵאתִי אֶת־הָעִיר אֶפְרֹשׂ אֶת־כַּפַּי אֶל־יְהוָה הַקֹּלוֹת יֶחְדָּלוּן וְהַבָּרָד לֹא יִהְיֶה־עוֹד לְמַעַן תֵּדַע כִּי לַיהוָה הָאָרֶץ (ke-tseti et-ha-ir, "as I go out of the city"; efros et-kappai el-YHWH, "I will spread my palms to YHWH"; ha-qolot yekhdolun, "the thunders will cease"; le-ma'an teda' ki la-YHWH ha-arets, "so that you may know that the land [belongs] to YHWH"). Mosheh goes outside the city to pray — perhaps because the city is polluted by idolatry or because open-air prayer is fitting for a storm. The spreading of palms (כַּפַּי, kappai) is a gesture of prayer/supplication. LXX: ὡς ἂν ἐξέλθω τὴν πόλιν, ἐκπετάσω τὰς χεῖράς μου πρὸς κύριον ("when I go out of the city, I will spread out my hands to the Lord").

**30** And you and your servants — I know that you do not yet fear from before YHWH God."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאַתָּה וַעֲבָדֶיךָ יָדַעְתִּי כִּי טֶרֶם תִּירְאוּן מִפְּנֵי יְהוָה אֱלֹהִים (ve-attah va-avadeicha yada'ti ki terem tire'un mippenei YHWH Elohim, "and you and your servants — I know that you do not yet fear from before YHWH God"). The word טֶרֶם (terem) = "not yet, before" — Mosheh acknowledges that their repentance is not genuine; they do not yet truly fear YHWH. LXX: καὶ σὺ καὶ οἱ θεράποντές σου, ἐπίσταμαι ὅτι οὐδέπω πεφόβησθε τὸν κύριον ("and you and your servants, I know that you have not yet feared the Lord").

**31** And the pishtah [flax] and the se'orah [barley] were struck, for the barley [was in] aviv [ear] and the flax [was in] giv'ol [bud].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהַפִּשְׁתָּה וְהַשְּׂעֹרָה נֻכָּתָה כִּי הַשְּׂעֹרָה אָבִיב וְהַפִּשְׁתָּה גִּבְעֹל (ve-ha-pishtah ve-ha-se'orah nukkatah, "and the flax and the barley were struck"; ki ha-se'orah aviv, "for the barley [was in] ear/head"; ve-ha-pishtah giv'ol, "and the flax [was in] bud/stalk"). The word אָבִיב (aviv) = "ear [of grain], green ear" — the same word as the month name Aviv/Abib (later Nisan). גִּבְעֹל (giv'ol) = "bud, stalk" — a rare word, perhaps related to גבעה (giv'ah, "hill") in the sense of a rounded bud. These are the early-ripening crops destroyed by the hail. LXX: τὸ δὲ λίνον καὶ ἡ κριθὴ ἐπλήγη ("and the flax and the barley were struck") — λίνον = "flax," κριθή = "barley."

**32** And the khittah [wheat] and the kussemeth [spelt] were not struck, for they [are] afilot [late/dark/hidden].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהַחִטָּה וְהַכֻּסֶּמֶת לֹא נֻכּוּ כִּי אֲפִילֹת הֵנָּה (ve-ha-khittah ve-ha-kussemeth lo nukku ki afilot hennah, "and the wheat and the spelt were not struck, for they [are] late/hidden"). The word אֲפִילֹת (afilot) = "late [in ripening], dark, hidden" — from אפל ("to be dark, late"). Because wheat and spelt ripen later than barley and flax, they had not yet emerged sufficiently to be destroyed. This detail explains why livestock and crops survive for the locust plague in ch. 10. LXX: ἡ δὲ πυρὸς καὶ ἡ ὄλυρα οὐκ ἐπλήγη· ὄψιμα γάρ ἐστιν ("and the wheat and the emmer were not struck, for they are late") — ὄψιμος = "late."

**33** And Mosheh went out from Par'oh, from the city, and he spread his palms to YHWH; and the thunders [qolot] and the hail ceased, and rain was not poured upon the land.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֵּצֵא מֹשֶׁה מֵעִם פַּרְעֹה אֶת־הָעִיר וַיִּפְרֹשׂ כַּפָּיו אֶל־יְהוָה וַיַּחְדְּלוּ הַקֹּלוֹת וְהַבָּרָד וּמָטָר לֹא־נִתַּךְ אָרְצָה (vayetse Mosheh me-im Par'oh et-ha-ir, "and Mosheh went out from Par'oh, from the city"; vayifros kappav el-YHWH, "and he spread his palms to YHWH"; vayakhdelu ha-qolot ve-ha-barad, "and the thunders and the hail ceased"; u-matar lo-nittakh artsah, "and rain was not poured upon the land"). The verb נִתַּךְ (nittakh) = niphal of נתך, "to pour, be poured" — a strong verb for torrential rain. LXX: καὶ ἐξῆλθεν Μωυσῆς ἀπὸ Φαραω ἐκτὸς τῆς πόλεως καὶ ἐξεπέτασεν τὰς χεῖρας πρὸς κύριον ("and Moses went out from Pharao, outside the city, and spread out his hands to the Lord").

**34** And Par'oh saw that the rain and the hail and the thunders [qolot] had ceased, and he continued to sin, and he made heavy [kaved] his heart — he and his servants.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּרְא פַּרְעֹה כִּי־חָדַל הַמָּטָר וְהַבָּרָד וְהַקֹּלֹת וַיֹּסֶף לַחֲטֹא וַיַּכְבֵּד לִבּוֹ הוּא וַעֲבָדָיו (vayar Par'oh ki-khadal ha-matar ve-ha-barad ve-ha-qolot, "and Par'oh saw that the rain and the hail and the thunders had ceased"; vayosef lakhato, "and he continued/added to sin"; vayakhbed libbo, "and he made heavy his heart"; hu va-avadav, "he and his servants"). **Hardening verb: kaved (כבד) = "make heavy." Hiphil וַיַּכְבֵּד — Par'oh is the agent.** After the hail stops, Par'oh reverts: he "continues to sin" (using the same root חטא that he confessed in v.27) and actively makes his own heart heavy again. The phrase הוּא וַעֲבָדָיו ("he and his servants") indicates the hardening is collective — both Par'oh and his court. LXX: καὶ ἰδὼν Φαραω ὅτι πέπαυται ὁ ὑετὸς καὶ ἡ χάλαζα καὶ αἱ φωναί, προσέθετο τοῦ ἁμαρτάνειν ("and Pharao, seeing that the rain and hail and voices had ceased, continued to sin"). καὶ ἐβάρυνεν τὴν καρδίαν αὐτοῦ ("and he made heavy his heart") — βαρύνω for kaved hiphil.

**35** And the heart of Par'oh was strengthened [chazaq], and he did not send out the sons of Yisra'el, as YHWH had spoken by the hand of Mosheh.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֶּחֱזַק לֵב פַּרְעֹה וְלֹא שִׁלַּח אֶת־בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל כַּאֲשֶׁר דִּבֶּר יְהוָה בְּיַד־מֹשֶׁה (vayekhezaq lev Par'oh, "and the heart of Par'oh was strengthened"; ve-lo shilakh et-benei Yisra'el, "and he did not send out the sons of Yisra'el"; ka-asher dibber YHWH be-yad Mosheh, "as YHWH had spoken by the hand of Mosheh"). **Hardening verb: chazaq (חזק) = "strengthen." Qal וַיֶּחֱזַק — Par'oh's heart strengthened itself.** The chapter ends with the standard qal formula, returning to the pattern of Par'oh's heart as subject. The phrase בְּיַד (be-yad, "by the hand of") indicates Mosheh as instrument/intermediary. LXX: καὶ ἐσκληρύνθη ἡ καρδία Φαραω ("and the heart of Pharao was hardened") — σκληρύνω passive for chazaq qal.

---

## Hardening-Verb Tracking — Exodus 9

| Verse | Hebrew Root | Verb Form | Translation | Agent |
|-------|-----------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| 9:2 | חזק (chazaq) | Hiphil ptc. מַחֲזִיק | "holding fast" | Par'oh (of people, not heart — but same root) |
| 9:7 | כבד (kaved) | Qal וַיִּכְבַּד | "was heavy" | Par'oh's heart (stative) |
| 9:12 | **חזק (chazaq)** | **Piel וַיְחַזֵּק** | **"strengthened"** | **YHWH (first divine hardening!)** |
| 9:34 | כבד (kaved) | Hiphil וַיַּכְבֵּד | "he made heavy" | Par'oh (self) |
| 9:35 | חזק (chazaq) | Qal וַיֶּחֱזַק | "was strengthened" | Par'oh's heart (self) |

### Cumulative Hardening-Verb Summary (Exodus 7–9)

| Verse | Hebrew Root | Verb Form | Translation | Agent |
|-------|-----------|-----------|-------------|-------|
| 7:3 | קשה (qashah) | Hiphil impf. אַקְשֶׁה | "I will make hard" | YHWH (predictive) |
| 7:13 | חזק (chazaq) | Qal וַיֶּחֱזַק | "was strengthened" | Par'oh's heart (self) |
| 7:14 | כבד (kaved) | Adjective כָּבֵד | "[is] heavy" | Par'oh's heart (state) |
| 7:22 | חזק (chazaq) | Qal וַיֶּחֱזַק | "was strengthened" | Par'oh's heart (self) |
| 8:15 | כבד (kaved) | Hiphil inf. abs. הַכְבֵּד | "he made heavy" | Par'oh (self) |
| 8:19 | חזק (chazaq) | Qal וַיֶּחֱזַק | "was strengthened" | Par'oh's heart (self) |
| 8:32 | כבד (kaved) | Hiphil וַיַּכְבֵּד | "he made heavy" | Par'oh (self) |
| 9:7 | כבד (kaved) | Qal וַיִּכְבַּד | "was heavy" | Par'oh's heart (stative) |
| **9:12** | **חזק (chazaq)** | **Piel וַיְחַזֵּק** | **"strengthened"** | **YHWH (FIRST ACTIVE DIVINE HARDENING)** |
| 9:34 | כבד (kaved) | Hiphil וַיַּכְבֵּד | "he made heavy" | Par'oh (self) |
| 9:35 | חזק (chazaq) | Qal וַיֶּחֱזַק | "was strengthened" | Par'oh's heart (self) |

**Pattern note — THE TURNING POINT:** Through Exodus 7–8, every hardening was either Par'oh's heart strengthening itself (chazaq qal) or Par'oh actively making his own heart heavy (kaved hiphil). In 9:12, for the first time in the narrative sequence, YHWH is the grammatical subject of the hardening verb in the piel (intensive/causative) stem: וַיְחַזֵּק יְהוָה אֶת־לֵב פַּרְעֹה ("and YHWH strengthened the heart of Par'oh"). This fulfills the predictions of 4:21 and 7:3. Remarkably, after this divine hardening (9:12), Par'oh still hardens himself again in 9:34 (kaved hiphil) — showing that divine and human agency interweave rather than divine action simply replacing human responsibility. In 9:34-35, both agencies appear together: Par'oh makes his heart heavy (v.34), and his heart is strengthened (v.35).

## Key Lexical Notes — Exodus 9

- **dever** (דֶּבֶר, vv. 3, 15): "pestilence, plague, murrain" — a deadly livestock disease. The nominal form from the root דבר (which also yields "word/thing"). LXX renders as θάνατος ("death"), emphasizing the lethal outcome.
- **shechin** (שְׁחִין, vv. 9–11): "boil, skin inflammation" — an eruption on the skin with ava'bu'ot ("blisters/pustules"). Appears later in the covenant curses (Devarim 28:27, 35) and on Iyyov (Job 2:7). LXX: ἕλκη φλυκτίδες ("sore blisters").
- **ava'bu'ot** (אֲבַעְבֻּעֹת, vv. 9-10): "blisters, pustules" — from a reduplicated root suggesting bubbling. A rare word appearing only in this plague account.
- **barad** (בָּרָד, vv. 18–34): "hail" — the most devastating plague to this point, combining ice, fire, and thunder. Described as כָּבֵד מְאֹד ("very heavy").
- **qolot** (קֹלֹת, vv. 23, 28, 29, 33, 34): "voices/thunders" — from קוֹל (qol). Thunder as the "voice" of God is a recurring motif (cf. Psalms 29). The phrase קֹלֹת אֱלֹהִים (qolot Elohim, v.28) = "thunders of God" — superlative usage.
- **piakh** (פִּיחַ, vv. 8, 10): "soot, fine ash" — from a kiln/furnace. The furnace of slavery becomes the instrument of plague.
- **chartummim** (חַרְטֻמִּים, v. 11): The magicians' final appearance — they are themselves struck by boils and cannot stand before Mosheh. Their progressive defeat: replicated blood (7:22) and frogs (8:7), failed at gnats (8:18), and now personally afflicted (9:11).
- **kaved** (כָּבֵד): This root saturates the chapter: the pestilence is "heavy" (v.3), the hail is "heavy" (vv. 18, 24), and Par'oh's heart is "heavy" or "made heavy" (vv. 7, 34). The weight of divine judgment mirrors the weight of Par'oh's stubbornness.
- **Goshen/Yisra'el distinction** (vv. 4, 6, 7, 26): Continues the theme from 8:22 — YHWH explicitly protects Yisra'el's livestock from dever (v.4, 6) and Goshen from hail (v.26). The verb הִפְלָה (hiflah, "set apart/distinguish," v.4) echoes 8:22.
- **mo'ed** (מוֹעֵד, v. 5): "appointed time" — YHWH sets precise timing for judgment, the same term used for Israel's sacred calendar and the Tent of Meeting.
- **he'emadtikha** (הֶעֱמַדְתִּיךָ, v. 16): "I caused you to stand/raised you up" — YHWH has sustained Par'oh for the purpose of displaying divine power and making YHWH's name known. Cited by Paul in Romans 9:17.
